name: Aperol Spritz
description: Italy's most iconic aperitivo — bittersweet Aperol, crisp Prosecco, and a splash of soda over ice. Light, low-ABV, and endlessly refreshing.
glassware: Wine glass or large rocks glass
steps: Fill a large wine glass with ice.
Add Prosecco first, then Aperol, then a splash of club soda.
Stir very gently once — just enough to combine without losing bubbles.
Garnish with a half-wheel of orange. Serve immediately.

mocktail.description: Swap Aperol for blood orange juice + a small splash of tonic (for bitterness), and Prosecco for sparkling water. Stir gently and garnish the same way.

name: Paloma
description: Mexico's most popular cocktail — tequila with grapefruit juice and a salted rim. Tart, slightly bitter, and more refreshing than a margarita.
steps: Run a grapefruit wedge around the rim of the glass and dip in salt.
Fill the glass with ice. Add tequila, grapefruit juice, lime juice, and agave syrup.
Top with club soda and stir gently once.
Garnish with a grapefruit wedge.

mocktail.description: Skip the tequila and increase grapefruit juice to 4 oz. Add a small splash of lemon juice for complexity. Everything else stays the same.

name: Hugo Spritz
description: A delicate Northern Italian spritz with elderflower liqueur, fresh mint, and Prosecco. Lighter and more floral than an Aperol Spritz — perfect for a hot afternoon.
source: classic Alpine aperitivo, originated in South Tyrol
steps: Fill a wine glass with ice.
Lightly slap the mint sprigs between your palms to release the oils, then add to the glass.
Add elderflower liqueur, then Prosecco, then a splash of soda.
Stir very gently once. Garnish with a lime slice and extra mint.

name: Classic Margarita
description: The gold standard — fresh lime juice, quality tequila, and Cointreau in perfect proportion. Shaken, not sour-mix, not frozen. The one to know by heart.
source: classic cocktail, proportions per Death & Co
steps: Optional: run a lime wedge around half the glass rim and dip in salt.
Combine tequila, Cointreau, and lime juice in a cocktail shaker with ice.
Shake hard for 10–12 seconds until the shaker is frosty cold.
Strain into a rocks glass over fresh ice (or into a chilled coupe without ice).
Garnish with a lime wheel.

name: Old Fashioned
description: The ur-cocktail — bourbon or rye, a sugar cube, bitters, and a twist. Three ingredients, zero shortcuts, maximum flavor. Based on the Serious Eats definitive recipe.

ingredients.name: demerara or simple syrup (or 1 sugar cube)

steps: Add syrup and bitters to a rocks glass. If using a sugar cube, muddle it with the bitters and a few drops of water until dissolved.
Add the whiskey and stir briefly.
Add one large ice cube (or several smaller ones). Stir for 20–30 seconds until well-chilled and slightly diluted.
Express the orange peel over the glass: hold it skin-side down over the drink and give it a firm twist to release the oils. Run the peel around the rim, then drop it in.

name: Negroni
description: Equal parts gin, Campari, and sweet vermouth — stirred, not shaken. Bitter, herbal, and unmistakably Italian. One of the great cocktails and one of the easiest to make.
source: classic Italian cocktail, proportions per Difford's Guide
steps: Combine gin, Campari, and sweet vermouth in a mixing glass filled with ice.
Stir for 30 seconds until well-chilled and diluted.
Strain into a rocks glass over a large ice cube.
Express the orange peel over the drink and drop it in as garnish.

name: Classic Mojito
description: Fresh mint, lime, rum, and soda — the definitive summer highball. Light, herbaceous, and genuinely refreshing. Based on the widely praised Difford's Guide recipe.

ingredients.name: mint sprig and lime wheel, for garnish

steps: Add mint leaves and simple syrup to a highball glass. Lightly press the mint with a muddler or bar spoon — just enough to bruise it and release the oils. Don't shred it.
Add lime juice and rum. Fill the glass with ice.
Top with club soda and stir gently once from bottom to top.
Garnish with a mint sprig (slap it once in your palm first) and a lime wheel.

mocktail.description: Skip the rum entirely and increase soda to 4 oz. Add an extra squeeze of lime and a few drops of coconut water for body. The mint-lime-soda combo is genuinely delicious on its own.

name: Red Wine Sangria
description: A Spanish classic made for a crowd — red wine with brandy, orange juice, and muddled fruit, steeped overnight for deep flavor. Makes a full pitcher. Based on the highly rated NYT Cooking recipe.
glassware: Wine glass or rocks glass over ice

ingredients.name: dry red wine (Rioja, Garnacha, or Merlot, 750ml bottle)
club soda (to top off when serving)

steps: Combine wine, brandy, orange juice, simple syrup, and all sliced fruit in a large pitcher. Stir well.
Cover and refrigerate at least 2 hours, ideally overnight — the longer it sits, the better the fruit infuses.
When ready to serve, taste and adjust sweetness with more syrup if needed.
Pour over ice-filled glasses and top each glass with a splash of club soda. Spoon in some fruit from the pitcher.

mocktail.description: Use dealcoholized red wine (e.g. Ariel Cabernet) or substitute with equal parts grape juice and cranberry juice. Skip the brandy and increase OJ to 6 oz. Everything else stays the same.

name: Watermelon Margarita
description: Fresh-blended watermelon juice shaken with tequila and lime — bright, juicy, and barely sweet without a drop of added sugar. Based on the Love & Lemons recipe.

ingredients.name: fresh watermelon juice (blend ~1.5 cups cubed watermelon and strain)
kosher salt or Tajín, for rim
lime wedge and small watermelon slice, for garnish

steps: Make the watermelon juice: blend cubed seedless watermelon until smooth, then strain through a fine-mesh sieve. You need about 3 oz per drink. Can be done up to a day ahead — store in the fridge.
Run a lime wedge around the rim of a rocks glass and dip in salt or Tajín.
Combine tequila, watermelon juice, lime juice, and Cointreau in a shaker with ice. Shake hard for 10 seconds.
Fill the glass with fresh ice and strain the margarita over it.
Garnish with a lime wedge and a small watermelon triangle on the rim.

mocktail.description: Skip the tequila and Cointreau. Use 5 oz watermelon juice, 1 oz lime juice, and a splash of orange juice for depth. Shake with ice and strain over a salted rim. Genuinely delicious.

name: Pitcher Margarita
description: A scaled-up version of the classic margarita built for a group — mix it ahead, keep it cold, and serve over ice. No shaking required. Tested ratio from Serious Eats.

ingredients.name: water (to account for dilution from shaking)

steps: Combine tequila, Cointreau, lime juice, and water in a large pitcher. Stir well. Taste — if it needs sweetness, stir in agave syrup.
Refrigerate until cold, at least 1 hour. Can be made up to 24 hours ahead.
When serving, salt the rims of glasses (optional), fill with ice, and pour the margarita mixture over.
Garnish each glass with a lime wheel. Give the pitcher a stir before each pour.
